Aberration.definition: An earthly creature warped into impossible anatomy — too many eyes and mouths, non-euclidean asymmetry.
Aberration.motifs: impossible non-euclidean anatomy, too many eyes or mouths in wrong places, a maddening asymmetry

Amphibian.definition: Frogs, toads, newts and salamanders — moist-skinned creatures at home in both water and on land.
Amphibian.motifs: moist glistening amphibian skin, webbed digits, a soft translucent throat sac

Aquatic.definition: Fish and sea-life of brine and deep water; creatures whose element is the ocean.
Aquatic.motifs: waterlogged fins and gill-slits, dripping brine, a bioluminescent deep-sea sheen

Arthropod.definition: Hard-shelled, many-legged invertebrates — crabs, scorpions, crustaceans; heavier and more armoured than Insect.
Arthropod.motifs: a hard segmented chitinous exoskeleton, jointed armored legs, twitching antennae

Avian.definition: Birds and winged fowl — feathered, beaked and taloned.
Avian.motifs: layered feathers, a hooked beak, taloned bird feet, folded or spread wings

Beast.definition: Ordinary flesh-and-blood animals — mammals and predators with no supernatural nature; the mundane baseline.
Beast.motifs: thick fur or hide, a fanged predatory muzzle, clawed paws and a feral posture

Celestial.definition: Holy, divine or angelic beings radiant with blessed light.
Celestial.motifs: a faint halo of pale holy light, gilded radiant markings, an otherworldly divine glow

Chimera.definition: A single creature fused from the mismatched parts of several different animals.
Chimera.motifs: mismatched body parts fused from several different animals, an unnatural hybrid silhouette

Construct.definition: A non-living thing assembled from inert material — stone, iron, bound parts — and animated; never organic.
Construct.motifs: a body assembled from inert material — stone, iron or bound components — with visible joints and seams, no organic softness

Demon.definition: An infernal fiend of hellfire, horns and cruelty — a creature of damnation.
Demon.motifs: infernal blackened flesh, jagged horns, burning ember eyes and a cruel predatory malice

Dragon.definition: Great scaled draconic reptiles with wings, horns and hoard-beast majesty; grander than a mere Reptile.
Dragon.motifs: heavy overlapping scales, membranous draconic wings, curved horns and a reptilian sneer

Eldritch.definition: A cosmic-horror entity from beyond reality — alien, unknowable and mind-breaking; not of this world, unlike an Aberration's earthly mutation.
Eldritch.motifs: cosmic-horror features that should not exist — extra eyes, writhing appendages, an alien wrongness

Elemental.definition: A being made of and wreathed in raw element — flame, stone, water, storm — living element, not a machine.
Elemental.motifs: its body made of and wreathed in raw element — flame, stone, water or storm — with glowing elemental energy at the seams

Fey.definition: A fae enchanter of glamour, illusion and luring — nature-spirit trickery worn on a living body; NOT a ghost (that is Spectral).
Fey.motifs: iridescent enchanted glamour-markings that shimmer like heat-haze, adornments of living flowers, petals or a bright hypnotic lure-light, and an uncanny too-flawless beguiling beauty built to deceive the eye

Flora.definition: Living, animate plant matter — bark, vine, leaf, root and blossom.
Flora.motifs: living plant matter — bark, vines, leaves, blossoms or roots — growing as part of its body

Fungal.definition: Mushroom, mold and spore organisms — caps, mycelium and drifting spores.
Fungal.motifs: clustered mushroom caps, spreading spores, mottled fungal growth and mycelial threads

Horror.definition: Nightmarish flesh built to disturb — exposed sinew, wrong proportions and body-horror dread of mundane origin.
Horror.motifs: nightmarish disturbing features, exposed sinew and wrong proportions built to unsettle

Insect.definition: Six-legged bugs — chitin, compound eyes, antennae and buzzing wings; smaller and lighter than Arthropod.
Insect.motifs: a chitinous insectoid body, compound eyes, thin multi-jointed legs and buzzing wings

Mollusk.definition: Soft, boneless bodies — tentacles, coiling shells and slick mucous; squid, snails and nautilus.
Mollusk.motifs: a soft boneless slick body, coiling tentacles or a spiral shell, a glistening mucous sheen

Ooze.definition: A formless semi-liquid blob — gelatinous, dripping, with no skeleton or fixed shape.
Ooze.motifs: a semi-liquid amorphous body that sags and drips, translucent gelatinous mass, no fixed skeleton

Parasite.definition: An organism that infests and feeds on hosts — burrowing growths that cling to and control other creatures.
Parasite.motifs: parasitic growths and burrowing organisms erupting from or clinging to its body

Reptile.definition: Cold-blooded scaled animals — lizards, crocodilians, turtles; mundane, unlike a Dragon's majesty or a Serpent's limbless coil.
Reptile.motifs: dry pebbled scales, cold slit-pupil eyes, clawed reptilian limbs

Serpent.definition: Limbless snakes — long, coiling, scaled bodies that glide and constrict.
Serpent.motifs: a long limbless coiling serpentine body, overlapping belly scales, a flicking forked tongue

Spectral.definition: The dead as pure spirit — ghosts, wraiths and incorporeal souls; translucent and dissolving, unlike Undead's rotting flesh.
Spectral.motifs: a translucent semi-transparent ghostly body, edges dissolving into vapor, a cold spectral glow

Undead.definition: Reanimated dead flesh and bone — corpses, skeletons and rot; corporeal, unlike incorporeal Spectral.
Undead.motifs: decayed rotting flesh over exposed bone, sunken hollow eye-sockets, a desiccated grave-touched look

Vampiric.definition: Blood-draining predators — pale, gaunt, fanged and hungry for blood.
Vampiric.motifs: gaunt pale predatory features, prominent fangs, blood-red accents

Void.definition: A being of light-swallowing nothingness — pure black unmaking, the wrongness that eats existence.
Void.motifs: a body of light-swallowing pure black void, an unstable outline edged in faint violet, wrongness that eats the surrounding light
